rationale: The doc exists and has a minimal audience section, but it hallucinates Mathlib module names (`Mathlib.Analysis.Calculus.RolleAxiomatised`, `Mathlib.Tactic.GhostFunction`) that do not exist in Mathlib, and entirely omits the mandatory specification structure (no acceptance criteria, no invariants, no test conditions, no goals/non-goals).
rubric_evidence: Mathlib.Analysis.Calculus.RolleAxiomatised — the axiomatised variant of Rolle's theorem that this module builds on.
Mathlib.Tactic.GhostFunction — the tactic for closing derivative-symbol-elimination goals.
Audience: 2nd-year graduate students in analysis. Required vocabulary: mean-value theorem, Rolle's theorem, derivative-on-an-interval.
